Fix pipefail error on node:20-slim by using bash shebang

commit.message:
[FIRRTL] Migrate all intrinsics to TableGen (#9847)

Move the 34 remaining manually-registered FIRRTL intrinsics into the
`FIRRTLIntrinsics.td` TableGen file, joining the 2 that were already
there. The circt-tblgen backend generates both the `lowering.add<>()`
registration calls and the Markdown documentation from these
definitions.

Remove the manual registration calls from FIRRTLIntrinsics.cpp, and
remove the manual documentation sections from FIRRTLIntrinsics.md.

Intrinsics that previously lacked documentation (LTL ops, clock_inv,
clock_div, mux cells, has_been_reset, fpga_probe) now have concise
descriptions with argument/result tables.

commit.message:
security: switch from HMAC to Ed25519 asymmetric signature

HMAC (symmetric) was insecure for open-source: the verification key
in source code IS the signing key, so anyone could forge licenses.

Ed25519 (asymmetric) fixes this:
- Public key embedded in source → can only VERIFY, not sign
- Private key stays with project owner → only they can generate keys
- Even with full source code access, forgery is impossible

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Day 101-102: Targeted Reconciliation (Claude Call 3) — module + pipeline integration, 70 tests

Day 101: storage/ai_reconcile.py (~637 LOC) — Claude Call 3 targeted reconciliation module. collect_flagged_items() filters/prioritizes items by tier (reject→low→medium, cap at 10), reconcile_flagged_items() sends image + flagged items to Claude for surgical review, merge_reconciled_items() merges corrections back (confirmed: +5 confidence, corrected: set 92, not_found: unchanged). Graceful skip on no flags/no API key/bad image, error handling returns originals. 34 tests.

Day 102: Wired reconciliation into run_ocr_and_make_draft() as pipeline Step 5 (OCR → Call 1 → Call 2 → Semantic → Call 3). Gate: only runs when semantic pipeline flagged items. Re-scores confidence after corrections, propagates corrected fields back to draft items. Debug payload: new targeted_reconciliation block. Pipeline metrics: STEP_CALL3_RECONCILE tracked in success/skip/fail. 36 tests, 1,705 total pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Build Real Dough Pizza Co. Sales CRM dashboard

Mobile-first Next.js app with three views:
- Dashboard with stat cards, weekly trend chart, SKU donut, and leaderboards
- Retailer breakdown with search, sort, and expandable store-level detail
- SKU breakdown with horizontal bar comparison and per-retailer drill-down

Uses Real Dough brand colors (navy, cream, SKU-specific pizza colors),
Roboto Condensed/Montserrat/Barlow typography, and card-based UI inspired
by the provided dashboard reference. Data sourced from Weekly Unit Sales
and Customer List Excel files.

Paper 103: The Asymptotic Penumbra — first Clinical Sub-Series (#237)

* README: highlight Papers 93–94 (Structural Vanishing + Mirror Quintic)

Bold the two landmark Hodge papers: P93 (universal trace vanishing
across all dimensions) and P94 (first CY3 middle cohomology CRM).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Paper 103: The Asymptotic Penumbra — first Clinical Sub-Series paper

First CRM paper in the medical domain. Applies constructive reverse
mathematics to the RCT statistical pipeline. The normal CDF Φ is the
transcendental gate requiring BISH+MP; the Asymptotic Penumbra
characterises the logical cost. Five theorems (A–E), three landmark
trials classified, 956 lines Lean (10 files, 58 theorems).

Includes: LaTeX + PDF (25pp), Lean bundle P103_RCTStatistics/,
README, metadata, master folder updates (paper list, roadmap,
handoff, booklet, GitHub README with Part Four: Clinical Sub-Series).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

  in both react_endpoint and dag_endpoint)

  always defined when the done payload is built
- Track fast-LLM usage during conversation summarization phase in dag_endpoint

  into the executor which already re-hydrates from the planner context)

commit.message:
feat: add Windows WSL2 uninstall script

🗑️ Complete Windows Uninstall Support

Based on user request: Now complete Windows system one-click uninstall script

New Features:

Windows Uninstall Script (windows-uninstall.ps1)
- PowerShell script for Windows
- Uninstall OpenClaw in WSL2 Ubuntu
- Optional: Remove config files and data
- Optional: Uninstall WSL2 and Ubuntu
- Optional: Disable WSL features
- Generate uninstall report
- User-friendly Chinese interface
- Administrator permission check

Features:

| Feature | Native | Docker | Windows |
|---------|--------|--------|---------|
| Stop services | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Remove service files | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Clean up config | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Remove data | ✅ Optional | ✅ Optional | ✅ Optional |
| Uninstall deps | ✅ Optional | ✅ Optional | ✅ Optional |
| Generate report | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Interactive confirm | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |

Usage:

Windows (PowerShell):
  .\windows-uninstall.ps1

Uninstall Process:

1. Check administrator permissions
2. Confirm uninstall operation
3. Check WSL2 and Ubuntu status
4. Uninstall OpenClaw in WSL2
5. Ask to remove config and data
6. Ask to uninstall WSL2 and Ubuntu
7. Ask to disable WSL features
8. Generate uninstall report

Safety Features:

- ✅ Interactive confirmation to prevent accidents
- ✅ Step-by-step prompts for precise control
- ✅ Option to keep important data
- ✅ Detailed uninstall report
- ✅ Reinstallation guidance
- ✅ Administrator permission check

Complete Lifecycle Management - All Platforms:

| Stage | Linux | macOS | Windows | Docker |
|-------|-------|-------|---------|--------|
| Deploy | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Troubleshoot | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Uninstall | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |

From deployment to uninstall, all platforms fully covered!

Documentation:
- Updated README.md with Windows uninstall instructions
- Updated README_CN.md with Windows uninstall instructions (Chinese)
- Updated CHANGELOG.md with complete platform coverage
- Updated file list

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Sentinel: Replace insecure Math.random usages with CSPRNG (#562)

* fix: Replace insecure Math.random() with cryptographically secure generators

This replaces various instances of `Math.random()` used to generate identifiers (SKUs, UUID fallbacks, event tracking IDs, review IDs, storage file names, order numbers, credit-direct session IDs) with `crypto.randomUUID()`, `crypto.getRandomValues()`, or `expo-crypto` equivalents. `Math.random()` is cryptographically weak and predictable, which poses a token predictability/collision vulnerability.

Co-authored-by: ogabasseyy <196925138+ogabasseyy@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Use CSPRNG for random identifiers

Replaced all instances of `Math.random()` used for identifier generation with cryptographically secure PRNG equivalents to prevent collision and prediction vulnerabilities.

- Web: used `crypto.randomUUID()` and `crypto.getRandomValues()`
- Mobile: used `expo-crypto`'s `Crypto.randomUUID()`

Co-authored-by: ogabasseyy <196925138+ogabasseyy@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: increase entropy for order number and SKU generation

Order suffix increased from 4 to 6 hex chars (16.7M variants vs 65K).
SKU suffix increased from 6 to 8 hex chars (4.3B variants vs 16.7M).
Strips UUID dashes before substring to use only hex chars.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove buggy Math.random fallback for UUIDs

- Removed fallback UUID function entirely in `variant-builder.tsx`, defaulting to `crypto.randomUUID()` to resolve ReferenceErrors where crypto is undefined without a fallback path.
- Verified missing `Crypto` import was handled in `ad-tracking.ts`.

Co-authored-by: ogabasseyy <196925138+ogabasseyy@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: strip UUID dashes before substring to preserve entropy

All randomUUID().substring() calls now strip dashes first to ensure
only hex chars are counted, preventing entropy regression vs the
original Math.random().toString(36) patterns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: google-labs-jules[bot] <161369871+google-labs-jules[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: ogabasseyy <196925138+ogabasseyy@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: make story join-reason choices actually affect the narrative

Previously only d1_before_club was overridden per join reason; all other
Day 1 phases (d1_arriving, d1_introductions, d1_settling) defaulted to
Sayori-centric scripts regardless of the player's selection, because the
phase instruction is the last (most influential) system message and
overrides the distant storyOptionsBlock context.

Add join-reason-specific phase instructions for every scripted Day 1 phase:
- d1_before_club: tighten monika override so she stays primary recruiter
- d1_arriving: Monika walks MC in / Yuri is flustered alone / Natsuki resets
  to neutral / self-invited stranger surprises everyone
- d1_introductions: each join reason colors how Yuri and Natsuki are met
- d1_settling: each join reason shapes who is most attentive to MC

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: add python-semantic-release for automated changelog and versioning (#7)

* ci: add release-please for automated changelog and versioning

Adds release-please GitHub Action that watches conventional commits on
main and opens a Release PR with changelog updates and version bumps.
Merging the Release PR creates a GitHub Release, which triggers the
existing publish.yml workflow to publish to PyPI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: switch to python-semantic-release for automated releases

Replace release-please with python-semantic-release. On every push to
main, semantic-release parses conventional commits, bumps the version in
pyproject.toml, updates CHANGELOG.md, creates a git tag and GitHub
Release. The existing publish.yml then fires to publish to PyPI.

No manual version bumps, changelog edits, or release creation needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------
